rows.runtimeCost: Existing service-backed matrix route plus the scoped PostgreSQL Structure DDL smoke from issue #868 and dense ERD screenshot smoke from issue #885. Job timeout remains 40 minutes.

rows.cacheImpact: Uses shared prepared Tauri binary and Rust target cache.
rows.supportClaimImpact: Runtime Happy Path evidence for the documented PostgreSQL slices only, including bounded table-plus-index Structure DDL, dense ERD render/search/selection/zoom/fit screenshot evidence, and the Safe Mode mode-dial matrix (toolbar toggle -> store -> gate -> dialog across strict/warn/off x prod/non-prod, issue #1124). ERD smoke does not claim FK row navigation, schema diff, migration impact, or data compare.

rows.runtimeCost: Enterprise RDBMS matrix route from #907 starts an on-demand SQL Server 2022 container only for the mssql spec, reuses the shared prepared Tauri binary, runs with max-parallel 1, and keeps the job timeout at 40 minutes.
rows.flakeRisk: SQL Server container startup/runtime risk is isolated to the enterprise matrix; seed is idempotent and the spec uses a production-tagged connection to cover confirmation paths.
rows.cacheImpact: Uses shared prepared Tauri binary and Rust target cache; no SQL Server service is started for non-MSSQL smoke rows.
rows.failureArtifacts: wdio report uploaded as e2e-smoke-artifacts-mssql; service startup logs are printed before failure.
rows.supportClaimImpact: Runtime Happy Path evidence for the bounded SQL Server catalog/query/cancel/tabular/edit-row slice only. This does not promote structured DDL, admin/security/jobs/users/roles, broad T-SQL parser/completion semantics, SQLCMD/meta-command scripting, import/export, profiler/activity, or full workbench parity.
rows.action: promote as blocking E2E for the bounded SQL Server runtime/edit slice

rows.runtimeCost: Reuses the enterprise RDBMS SQL Server 2022 container and canonical dbo seed from the mssql route (max-parallel 1, shared prepared Tauri binary, 40-minute job timeout); adds one more matrix leg on the same on-demand container.
rows.flakeRisk: Same SQL Server container startup/runtime risk as the mssql route; the spec only reads the schema tree so it adds no write/state-bleed risk.
rows.cacheImpact: Uses shared prepared Tauri binary and Rust target cache; no extra service beyond the mssql enterprise container.
rows.failureArtifacts: wdio report uploaded as e2e-smoke-artifacts-mssql-schema-filter; MSSQL service startup logs print before failure.
rows.supportClaimImpact: Runtime Happy Path evidence that USER_SCHEMAS_SQL hides fixed database-role and guest schemas while dbo still renders; no other MSSQL capability is claimed.
rows.action: keep blocking E2E for the schema-list filter regression

rows.runtimeCost: Enterprise RDBMS matrix route from #907 starts an on-demand Oracle XE 21 slim-faststart container only for the oracle spec, reuses the shared prepared Tauri binary, runs with max-parallel 1, and keeps the job timeout at 40 minutes.
rows.flakeRisk: Oracle container startup/runtime risk is isolated to the enterprise matrix; the service-name fixture stays bounded to XEPDB1 and the spec avoids structured DDL/raw-admin/PLSQL routine claims.
rows.cacheImpact: Uses shared prepared Tauri binary and Rust target cache; no Oracle service is started for non-Oracle smoke rows.
rows.failureArtifacts: wdio report uploaded as e2e-smoke-artifacts-oracle; service startup logs are printed before failure.
rows.supportClaimImpact: Runtime Happy Path evidence for the bounded Oracle service-name catalog/query/cancel/tabular/edit-row and Safe Mode confirmation slice only. This does not promote SID/TNS/wallet/TLS/advanced auth, structured DDL, raw DDL/admin, full parser/completion promotion, PL/SQL body/package authoring/source, trigger catalog, import/export, profiler/activity, users/roles/grants/session/storage, or full Oracle semantics.
rows.action: promote as blocking E2E for the bounded Oracle service-name runtime/edit slice

rows.runtimeCost: Existing embedded DuckDB matrix route; no external service startup. The spec seeds its own file from e2e/fixtures/duckdb/schema-filter/seed.sql via prepareDuckdbFixture (core.users populated, main left empty), so no seed-smoke external target is used. Job timeout remains 40 minutes.
rows.flakeRisk: Lower than service-backed rows; isolated local DuckDB file. The flat DuckDB tree has no per-namespace label DOM, so the spec verifies the fix by counting the single 'No tables' placeholder; the pre-fix duplicate-main key reconcile is a known observation point on first integrated run.
rows.cacheImpact: Uses shared prepared Tauri binary and Rust target cache.
rows.supportClaimImpact: Runtime Happy Path evidence that list_namespaces hides the internal system/temp catalogs so the empty main renders once, while a real user schema still renders its table; no other DuckDB capability is claimed.
rows.action: keep blocking E2E for the schema-list filter regression
